distribution_plot.caption: Distribution of 5 categories across 5,630 customers. Horizontal bar chart chosen for readability at this category count.

stacked_churn_plot.caption: Each bar spans 100% of customers inside that category. Red = churn rate. Orange dashed line = overall retention baseline — any category whose red segment extends PAST this line churns more than average (a hotspot).

chi_squared.narration: χ² = 51.83 on 4 df. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained.

cramers_v.narration: Cramér's V = 0.096 — weak association with churn.

narrative.distribution: 'preferred_payment_mode' has 5 category/ies across 5,630 customers. Most common: 'Debit Card' (41.1%). Least common: 'UPI' (7.4%).
narrative.churn_rates: Overall churn rate = 16.8%. Highest-churn category: 'COD' at 24.9% (1.48× the baseline). Lowest-churn category: 'Credit Card' at 14.2% (0.84× the baseline).
narrative.association: χ² = 51.83 on 4 df. Highly significant (p < 0.0001). Tests whether the category proportions differ between churn and retained. Cramér's V = 0.096 — weak association with churn.

recommendation.rationale: Weak association (Cramér's V = 0.096). One-hot encode for completeness but deprioritise in feature selection.
